kicker: PT LEVEL I // FROM RAILROAD TO AEROSPACE

beats.narration: Welcome to penetrant testing: an amplifier for your eye, turning a crack far too tight to see into a bold, unmistakable line.

payload.title: PENETRANT TESTING
payload.sub: Surface-breaking flaws, made impossible to miss.

beats.narration: Eighteen hundreds: railroads soak axles in oil, dust them with chalk, watch stains bleed out. Magnetic particle eclipses it. Then wartime aluminum brings it back.

payload.heading: From the railroad shop to aerospace

beats.narration: Three moves, unchanged since the chalk days. Penetrant creeps in by capillary action. Wipe the surface. Developer pulls it back out, far wider than the flaw.

payload.heading: Penetrant, removal, developer
payload.note: CAPILLARY IN → WIPE CLEAN → BLEED OUT WIDE

beats.narration: PT finds cracks, laps, and surface porosity on nearly any nonporous solid, magnetic or not. But the flaw must reach the surface, and stay clean.

chips.t: Must be open to the surface

beats.narration: So remember the two non-negotiables: the flaw must be open to the surface, and that opening must be clean. Everything ahead serves those two facts.

payload.sub: Two facts the whole method rests on.
